request: spec this new export-to-CSV feature before I start building it
rationale: when_to_use: before substantive implementation on a non-trivial feature — the canonical positive trigger.

request: /spec csv-export
rationale: explicit slash invocation named in when_to_use.

request: fix the typo in the README heading
rationale: description NOT clause: not for trivial/test/doc-only edits (the gate scopes those out).

request: the plan is approved, now execute it
rationale: description NOT clause: not for executing an already-approved plan (that is /supervise) — cross-skill discriminator.

baseline_lacks: an ad-hoc reply with no persisted spec/plan artifacts on disk
rationale: description: brainstorm a feature into spec.md + plan.md under .agent/plans/<slug>/.

assertion: the spec contains explicit, verifiable acceptance criteria
baseline_lacks: a vague feature description with no testable success bar
rationale: upstream planning-discipline — the spec-gate exists to force a checkable plan before edits.

assertion: routes to plan approval instead of editing code directly
rationale: description: then route to plan approval; enforced by the spec-gate tool boundary.

request: the plan is written — execute it
rationale: when_to_use: user has a written plan and says execute / run the plan.

request: /supervise csv-export full auto
rationale: when_to_use names the slash form and 'full auto'.

request: write the plan for the CSV export feature
rationale: description NOT clause: not for writing the plan itself (that is /spec) — cross-skill discriminator.

request: just bump the version string in package.json
rationale: description NOT clause: not for a single small edit with no waves — just make the edit.

assertion: dispatches the plan to specialist agents wave by wave
baseline_lacks: doing all the work inline in one context with no agent dispatch
rationale: description: dispatch a multi-wave plan to specialist agents.

assertion: runs an audit and aborts on a risk-area violation
baseline_lacks: no completion audit and no abort condition
rationale: description: with audit + risk-area abort.

assertion: under --goal-mode it enforces a run budget / attempt cap
rationale: description: supports --goal-mode for budgeted runs.

request: the builder says the wave is done — check it before we accept it
rationale: when_to_use: a builder reported complete and you need a second independent check.

request: /verify-completion wave-2-claim
rationale: explicit slash invocation in when_to_use.

request: review this function for naming and style
rationale: description NOT clause: not for code-style review (that is a reviewer's lane).

request: I just wrote this in this same session — confirm it's done
rationale: description NOT clause: not for verifying work you authored in the same context — must be a fresh spawn.

assertion: runs in a separate/fresh context, not the author's
baseline_lacks: the author self-approving in their own context
rationale: description: independently re-verify in a separate context; the verifier must be a fresh spawn.

assertion: combines mechanical evidence (files/tests/assertions) with a refute-by-default semantic judge
baseline_lacks: an unstructured 'looks good to me' with no evidence
rationale: description: mechanical evidence plus a refute-by-default semantic judge.

baseline_lacks: free-form prose with no gateable verdict object
rationale: description: emits the shared verdict schema (docs/scoring-convention.md).

request: ship this — commit and open a PR
rationale: when_to_use: user asks to commit and open a PR — 'ship this'.

request: /wrap
rationale: explicit slash invocation in when_to_use.

request: wrap up — though I haven't made any changes yet
rationale: description NOT clause: not when nothing has changed yet — there is nothing to commit.

request: review my changes for correctness
rationale: description NOT clause: not for review or verification (run those before wrapping).

assertion: runs gitleaks + risk-area guards before committing
baseline_lacks: committing with no secret scan or risk-area guard
rationale: description: runs gitleaks + risk-area guards before committing.

assertion: commits the staged changes and opens a PR
baseline_lacks: a local commit with no PR opened
rationale: description: commits staged changes and opens a PR.

assertion: push is user-confirmed by default (--auto-push is opt-in)
baseline_lacks: pushing to the remote without confirmation
rationale: description: push is user-confirmed by default; --auto-push and --auto-merge are opt-in.

request: is the harness passing its own gates right now?
rationale: when_to_use: a health check of the harness itself — 'is the harness passing its own gates'.

request: /harness-audit
rationale: explicit slash invocation in when_to_use.

request: audit my app's own codebase for bugs
rationale: description NOT clause: not for auditing a consumer project's own codebase (use that project's test suite).

request: the harness is failing one of its gates — go in and fix whatever is broken so it passes
rationale: description NOT clause: observes and reports only. A pure fix/apply request (no 'audit' framing) routes to a builder, not the read-only auditor — note a request that BOTH says 'audit the harness' AND asks to fix still triggers harness-audit, which audits and defers the fix, so the discriminating negative is a fix-only request.

assertion: runs the machine integrity layer once as a dry-run
baseline_lacks: an opinion on harness health with no gate execution
rationale: description: run the machine integrity layer once as a dry-run.

assertion: presents a per-check pass/fail table and cites the P1-1 doc-reality result
baseline_lacks: a prose summary with no per-check breakdown or doc-reality citation
rationale: description: present a per-check pass/fail table, cite the P1-1 doc-reality result.

assertion: observes only — for any failure gives root-cause + fix + backlog follow-up without applying a change
baseline_lacks: silently editing files to fix a failing check
rationale: description: it observes and reports only; for any failure give root-cause + fix + backlog follow-up.
